title: Fix browse NetSuite binary UX: false timeouts, login/query preconditions, popup-select handling
description:
`browse` binary reports valid NetSuite actions as failures and exposes bad UX on common preconditions. Repro from SO 392279 in sandbox 5582598-sb2 during PR #23 workflow testing cost the test agent ~10+ minutes of false-failure cycles before usable work began.

## False-timeout / state-racing issues (mid-run)
- `ns navigate` timed out on valid Sales Order create/view pages
- `ns save` timed out after the record successfully saved
- Workflow-button click timed out after the workflow state had already changed
- Popup-select fields required frame/`setTarget` hacks instead of working through the normal command flow

## Precondition / validation UX issues (early-run)
- `ns login --account 5582598_SB2` failed with 'Account not found in auth config. Available: 5582598-sb2:account2, 5582598-sb2:account3'. Needs fuzzy match or a 'did you mean' suggestion — exact-match on account-config keys is brittle.
- `ns query` returned 'NetSuite client API (nlapiGetField) not available on this page' 5+ times when run before opening a NS record page. Bad error for a precondition — should either auto-navigate or fail with a human message like 'open a NetSuite record page first'.
- `ns navigate salesorder` (short form) failed with 'Cannot navigate to invalid URL'. Either accept short-form record types or print the expected URL shape.

## Root cause
Binary's wait/record-page handling around NetSuite navigations, workflow submits, and helper-frame pickers treats success-with-slow-DOM as failure. Separately, the login/query/navigate CLI surface is strict where it should be forgiving and silent where it should be informative.

## Acceptance
- `ns navigate` works on Sales Order create/view pages (both long URL and short record-type forms)
- `ns save` returns success when the record saves (no false timeout after DOM settles)
- Workflow button clicks return success when the state changes
- Popup-select fields work without frame hacks
- `ns login` fuzzy-matches account keys or suggests closest match on mismatch
- `ns query` either auto-opens a NS page or fails with a clear precondition message
- `netsuite-browse` SKILL.md in the netsuite repo updated to document: exact login account-name format, that `ns query` requires an open NS record page, and `ns navigate` URL/syntax requirements — at least until the binary-side UX fixes ship

## Context
Tracked from netsuite repo PR #23 (`feature/sales-order-approval`), sandbox 5582598-sb2, SO 392279. Test agent session transcript: `~/.codex/sessions/2026/04/15/rollout-2026-04-15T23-57-23-019d946f-bb55-7ae1-8b5e-d41c0a4c1342.jsonl`

title: Wire codex Stop hook into installer and hooks.json
description: Codex Stop hook exists but completion still falls back to transcript polling. Find why ~/.codex/hooks/codex-stop.sh is missing, ensure Stop is registered in ~/.codex/hooks.json, and verify agent-eventsd gets fast completion evidence from the hook.
notes: Verified SharedCodexHookSymlinks() is already consumed by both cmd/steez/install.go and internal/updater/update.go. The missing ~/.codex/hooks/codex-stop.sh was stale local install state: the hook was added in runtime_assets, but the installer had not been rerun, and make install only rebuilds the CLI. Kept ~/.codex/hooks.json user-managed, added install-time guidance plus doctor coverage for Codex hook symlinks, fixed codex-stop.sh to emit JSON continue:true, and aligned Stop guidance with official Codex hooks docs (no matcher override, no async). Live smoke: fresh ren-codex pane %875 resolved working->idle 454ms after DONE; watch record af240c76-8a09-4cc7-9c35-675824caf38f stayed out of degraded mode (degraded_since_ms null, last_reconcile_ms null), proving hook-driven fast evidence.
close_reason: Codex Stop fast-evidence path verified end-to-end; stale local symlink fixed, user hooks.json guidance corrected, and live ren-codex smoke passed.

title: fast-evidence observer: Codex transcript-append producer
description:
## Why

Slice 3 lands the tailer and Claude resolver. Codex needs parity — same near-real-time notification guarantee on the healthy path, same hookless producer pattern (per steez-2wk spec non-goal).

## What

Add a Codex-specific resolver that maps a pane to its Codex transcript path. Reuse the generic tailer and fs-event shim from slice 3.

### Design

1. **Codex resolver.** `_eventsd_resolve_transcript` gets a `codex` branch. Map pane session identity to Codex's on-disk transcript location (verify at implementation — Codex may not expose a session id the same way Claude does via `@session_id`; fallback is screen-hash against tmux capture, but transcript-append is preferred).
2. **Tailer reuse.** No new tailer. Resolver returns path; existing watcher registers it.
3. **Parity.** `ren-codex` model uses the same resolver (it's Codex under the hood).

## Strict TDD (inherits steez-yu9 rules)

1. Red: `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-agent-eventsd-fast-codex.sh` — launch fake codex that writes a real transcript, degraded-fallback disabled, assert notification arrives <2s after idle.
2. Green: add codex resolver branch.
3. Refactor: if slice 3 left the tailer coupled to claude-specific assumptions, generalize.
4. Verifier PASS.
5. Agent harness green.

## Acceptance

- Codex red test fails on main (even after slice 3 — resolver branch missing).
- Red test passes after green.
- <2s latency bound holds.
- Both claude and codex tests green in the same run — no mutual regression.
- ren-codex covered by the same resolver.
close_reason: Superseded by hook-based fast-evidence plan (same rationale as steez-hsu). Codex parity lands through the codex-session-start.sh Stop hook (gated on codex_hooks=true), not through a shared transcript tailer. Replacement path: steez-2mn (evidence CLI, done) + follow-up bead for codex Stop hook wiring.

title: negative test: unknown agent falls through to degraded-fallback
description:
## Why

Slices 3 and 4 add a fast-evidence resolver that dispatches on agent type. An accidental future change could hard-require a resolver match, silently breaking panes where the agent is unknown (SSH shells, new agent CLIs, boot race before `@session_id` is set). Degraded-fallback must remain the correctness valve.

## What

A negative test that locks in: when `_eventsd_resolve_transcript` returns empty, the watch still resolves via degraded-fallback within the documented bound.

## Strict TDD (inherits steez-yu9 rules)

1. Red: `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-agent-eventsd-fallback-unknown-agent.sh` — arm a watch against a pane whose agent type does not match any resolver, let fast-path do nothing, assert notification arrives via degraded-fallback within `SILENCE_WINDOW_MS + RECONCILE_INTERVAL_MS + epsilon`.
2. Green: likely passes as-is once slice 3 lands — the fallback already exists (`_eventsd_reconcile`, `watch_tick`).
3. If green on first run: still land the test. Purpose is regression prevention, not new behavior.
4. Verifier PASS.
5. Agent harness green.

## Acceptance

- Test asserts both that fast-path stays silent for unknown agents AND that degraded-fallback still fires.
- Fulfills rule #4 from `specs/agent-events.md` Verification requirements (fallback companion test).
- Guards against a future refactor that accidentally makes the resolver required.

title: fast-evidence observer: Claude transcript-append producer
description:
## Why

Primary path to notification latency. Without a real fast-evidence producer, every armed watch falls to degraded-fallback (30s silence window + 5s reconcile). This is the slice that makes notifications actually near-real-time on the healthy path.

## What

Inside `agent-eventsd`, add a singleton fs-event tailer that resolves a Claude pane's transcript path from its session id and emits `source=fast` evidence on every append.

### Design (locked from retro discussion)

1. **Resolver.** `_eventsd_resolve_transcript <pane>` dispatches on the `agent` field from `agent-state`. Claude resolver maps pane `@session_id` -> `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<session_id>.jsonl` (or wherever the transcript lives — verify at implementation time). Unknown agent returns empty; falls through to degraded-fallback.
2. **Single tailer.** One fswatch/inotifywait coprocess inside the service, holding `{path -> [watch_id,...]}`. Armed watches register path on `watch.start`; remove on watch close. Portable shim `_eventsd_fs_tail` detects which tool is available.
3. **Evidence emission.** On append event, look up all watches keyed to that path and stamp evidence via the existing ingestion path (`--source fast`). Consumers already handle that source tag (see `shared/steez/bin/agent-eventsd:834`).
4. **No hook.** Per spec non-goal (steez-2wk). Hookless, model-agnostic where possible, model-specific only at the resolver boundary.

## Strict TDD (inherits steez-yu9 rules)

1. Red: `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-agent-eventsd-fast-claude.sh` — launch fake claude that writes a real JSONL transcript, degraded-fallback disabled via env (`AGENT_EVENTSD_DISABLE_FALLBACK=1` or equivalent), assert notification arrives **in <2s** after agent idles. Red must fail on current main because no fast producer exists.
2. Green: smallest implementation to pass — resolver + tailer + fast-evidence emission.
3. Refactor: tighten tailer abstraction if resolver can be reused in slice 4.
4. Verifier PASS.
5. Agent harness green.

## Acceptance

- Red test fails on main. Fails for the right reason (no fast producer), not missing deps.
- Red test passes after green.
- Latency bound asserted: <2s from agent idle to notification delivery.
- Fast path works with degraded-fallback disabled (proves producer exists independently of fallback).
- Existing tests still green — no regression in degraded-fallback, supersession, pending timeout, or delivery idempotency.
close_reason: Superseded by hook-based fast-evidence plan. The transcript-tailer approach (fswatch/inotifywait coprocess + path->watch registry + per-agent resolver) was rejected in architectural review on 2026-04-15 in favor of Claude/Codex native turn-end hooks calling `agent-eventsd evidence` directly. The cited 'no hooks on primary path' non-goal (steez-2wk) was not grounded in a concrete failure and was dropped. Replacement path: steez-2mn (evidence CLI, done) + follow-up bead for permission-state.sh hook wiring.

title: specs/agent-events.md: add Verification requirements section
description:
## Why

steez-401 shipped with the fake-agent harness playing the role of the fast-evidence producer the service was meant to observe. No real-world producer was ever wired. Related failures from the retro:

1. Spec described fast evidence in ambient language ("events come from transcript append, screen refresh, or both"), not as a normative MUST.
2. No latency bound in the red test — 30s degraded-fallback satisfied "exactly one notification."
3. Graceful degradation hid the missing primary path; no test ever disabled the fallback.
4. Harness was both SUT orchestrator and evidence producer — circular.

## What

Add a `## Verification requirements` section to `specs/agent-events.md` with four normative rules:

1. **Producer presence.** Every event producer named in this spec MUST have a production invocation site and a test that exercises it on the real runtime path. Mocking the producer in a test harness does not satisfy this.
2. **Latency bounds.** Any real-time behavior named in this spec (notification dispatch, evidence ingestion, reconcile cycle) MUST have a red test that asserts a latency bound, not only a correctness claim.
3. **Harness isolation.** The test harness MUST NOT produce the evidence the service is meant to observe. Evidence must come from an independent producer (real agent, fs-event emitter, or dedicated fixture that is not the SUT's runtime).
4. **Fallback companion tests.** Every fallback path (degraded reconcile, pending timeout, etc.) MUST have a companion test that disables the fallback and asserts the healthy path stands alone.

## Strict TDD

Documentation lockdown slice — no red/green code. Sequence:

1. Draft the section in `specs/agent-events.md`.
2. Cross-reference from `specs/README.md` so the rules are discoverable.
3. Verifier subagent reads the new section and confirms each rule is precise enough to derive a test from.
4. Close.

## Why this slice blocks 3-5

Slices 3 (fast-claude), 4 (fast-codex), and 5 (unknown-agent fallback negative test) must inherit these rules in their red tests. Landing this first means the next beads are written to the new bar.

## Acceptance

- `specs/agent-events.md` has a `## Verification requirements` section with the four rules above, each phrased as MUST.
- `specs/README.md` links to it.
- Verifier PASS: each rule is actionable and test-derivable.
close_reason:
Verifier VERDICT: PASS on first iteration — all four rules derivable to red tests without re-reading steez-401 retro; no tightening needed.

Changes:
- specs/agent-events.md:300-310 — new ## Verification requirements section with four MUST rules (Producer presence, Latency bounds, Harness isolation, Fallback companion tests), each with RFC 2119 MUST and a steez-401 failure-mode citation.
- specs/README.md:79 — new bullet #7 under Key Architectural Rules cross-referencing #verification-requirements.

Commit: d019224 docs: add Verification requirements to specs/agent-events.md

Downstream: steez-hsu (Claude transcript-append producer), steez-s5e (Codex producer), and steez-k9p (unknown-agent fallback negative test) are now unblocked and MUST write red tests derived from these four rules.

title: installer manifest contract: assert runtime bin symlinks cover every agent-* script dep
description:
## Why

`agent-send` shells out to `$HOME/.steez/bin/agent-eventsd prearm` but that symlink was missing (`agent-eventsd` absent from `internal/installer/runtime_assets.go`). The prearm call is guarded by `|| true`, so WATCH_ID stayed empty, no watch registered, and notifications never fired end-to-end. Users had to fall entirely through degraded-fallback to get any ping at all.

## What

Add a regression test that parses every `shared/steez/bin/agent-*` script for `$HOME/.steez/bin/<tool>` references and asserts each referenced tool is listed in `SharedBinSymlinks()` in `internal/installer/runtime_assets.go`. Test runs against the manifest, not a live install — no symlink mutation.

## Strict TDD

1. Red: `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-runtime-manifest-contract.sh` (or Go unit test against the runtime_assets package). Must fail on current main because `agent-eventsd` is referenced in `agent-send` and `agent-watch` but missing from the manifest.
2. Green: add `{Name: "agent-eventsd", RelPath: "shared/steez/bin/agent-eventsd"}` to `SharedBinSymlinks()`. (Edit already staged locally — commit as part of green.)
3. Refactor: none expected.
4. Verifier subagent PASS with the red test and the diff.
5. Agent test harness green before close.

## Acceptance

- Red test fails on main before the manifest edit.
- Red test passes after the edit.
- Future agent-* scripts that shell to a new `$HOME/.steez/bin/<tool>` will fail the same test if the tool is not added to the manifest.

## Files

- `internal/installer/runtime_assets.go` — add entry.
- `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-runtime-manifest-contract.sh` — new test (or Go equivalent under `internal/installer/`).
- `shared/steez/bin/agent-send`, `shared/steez/bin/agent-watch` — referenced by test, not edited.

title: security: /cookie-picker GET serves bearer token in page source without auth
notes:
src/core/cookie-picker-routes.ts:75-81 serves HTML with the bearer token inlined via src/core/cookie-picker-ui.ts:333 (const AUTH_TOKEN = '${authToken || ""}'). No auth check on the GET.

Exploit chain:
1. User visits malicious page in headed mode (port hardcoded to 34567 at cli.ts:572).
2. Malicious page does fetch('http://127.0.0.1:34567/cookie-picker') — same-origin if target ever redirects via 127.0.0.1:34567.
3. Page parses the HTML, extracts AUTH_TOKEN.
4. Page posts to /sidebar-command with the token → spawns Claude with Bash,Read,Glob,Grep,Write in the user's git worktree.

Fixes (any one):
- Require Bearer token on GET /cookie-picker.
- Issue a short-lived scoped token for picker-only operations.
- Randomize the headed-mode port (currently hardcoded 34567).
- Add Origin/Referer check on POST endpoints.

Tie-in: emitted by audit steez-ltc.
Likely obsolete — /cookie-picker and /sidebar-command are both being deleted by steez-bql. The token-leak → local-CSRF path disappears with the endpoints. Reassess after bql lands: if endpoints gone, close as obsolete; if any residual surface remains, harden it.
close_reason: obsolete: /cookie-picker and /sidebar-command endpoints deleted in steez-bql

title: fix: package.json test scripts silently match zero files (bun 1.3.5)
notes:
This is the REAL invisibility mechanism behind the months-long steez-rmb latency — not non-TTY output suppression.

Verified: bun 1.3.5 treats directory arguments to bun test as filter patterns, not paths. When the filter matches zero files, bun silently exits 0 with ONLY 'bun test v1.3.5 (1e86cebd)' as output.

All three package.json scripts are broken this way:
- test:       bun test src/core/test/ src/ns/test/ --ignore '**/*e2e*'
- test:core:  bun test src/core/test/
- test:ns:    bun test src/ns/test/

Reproduction:
  cd shared/steez/browse
  bun test src/core/test/     # exit 0, empty output
  bun test                    # exit 0, empty output
  cd src/core/test && bun test # WORKS: discovers tests, proper output + exit code

Workarounds tried:
- './src/core/test/' prefix — still silent
- 'src/core/test/*.test.ts' (unquoted) — silent exit 0 (shell-expanded files treated as non-matching filters)
- 'src/core/test/*.test.ts' (quoted) — correctly errors 'did not match any test files' and exits 1

Fix options:
1. cd into each dir: 'cd src/core/test && bun test' — ergonomic but loses --ignore
2. List files explicitly via build-time glob
3. Upgrade bun and re-test
4. Use bunfig.toml to set test.root

Acceptance: bun test command that (a) runs every *.test.ts under src/core/test/ and src/ns/test/, (b) skips *e2e*, (c) exits non-zero on any failure, (d) prints visible failure details. Fixing the test paths from the sibling bead alone does NOT restore visibility — it just means the broken tests now exit 1, but the package.json wrapper still masks it.

Tie-in: emitted by audit steez-ltc.
Chosen approach: option 4. Add a small scripts/test-files.sh runner that enumerates *.test.ts under the requested test roots, excludes *e2e*, and passes explicit file args to bun test. Reason: smallest durable fix that avoids the bun 1.3.5 directory/filter trap, keeps e2e exclusion, and preserves real failure output + exit codes.
Follow-up: one bun test invocation with the full explicit file list still exited 0 and stopped after the early core files, so the runner now executes bun test once per matched file and ORs the exit codes. That preserves visible failure output and makes any single-file failure fail the script.
close_reason: Approach 4 shipped: added shared/steez/browse/scripts/test-files.sh and pointed package.json test/test:core/test:ns at it. The runner finds *.test.ts under the requested roots, skips *e2e*, runs bun test once per file, and returns non-zero if any file fails. Live-source check: bun run test now exits 1 and surfaces the current sidebar failures in src/core/test/sidebar-agent-roundtrip.test.ts and src/core/test/sidebar-integration.test.ts with 'Server did not start in time'. Adversarial checks: with a temporary failure in src/core/test/activity.test.ts, bun run test exited 1 and printed the Expected false / Received true failure plus the live sidebar failures; bun run test:core also exited 1 and printed the same injected failure plus the live sidebar failures. With a temporary failure in src/ns/test/errors.test.ts, bun run test:ns exited 1 and printed the injected failure. Both temporary monkey-patches were reverted before close.

title: fix: 3 stale ../src/ paths in sidebar tests (same class as steez-rmb)
notes:
Same class of drift steez-rmb fixed, but three paths were missed. Confirmed failing:

- src/core/test/sidebar-integration.test.ts:40 — path.resolve(__dirname, '..', 'src', 'server.ts') → src/core/src/server.ts (doesn't exist). beforeAll throws 'Server did not start in time' after 15s.
- src/core/test/sidebar-agent-roundtrip.test.ts:76 — same bug.
- src/core/test/sidebar-agent-roundtrip.test.ts:107 — path.resolve(__dirname, '..', 'src', 'sidebar-agent.ts') → src/core/src/sidebar-agent.ts (doesn't exist).

Fix: drop the 'src' segment. Correct path from src/core/test/ is path.resolve(__dirname, '..', 'server.ts') and '..', 'sidebar-agent.ts'.

Verified: bun test src/core/test/sidebar-integration.test.ts fails with 'Server did not start in time' after 15s.

Tie-in: emitted by audit steez-ltc.
Likely obsolete — sidebar-integration.test.ts and sidebar-agent-roundtrip.test.ts are both being deleted by steez-bql. Reassess after bql lands: if files gone, close as obsolete; if somehow kept, fix the 3 stale ../src/X imports.

- Implementation follows the proposed spec in specs/agent-events.md
- Existing watch semantics remain intact

title: Engineer agent-watch lifecycle for reliable notifications
description:
The current agent-watch startup path relies on a blind nohup from agent-watch add. That works inconsistently across launchers and fails under Codex exec_command, leaving watches queued with no daemon polling them.

Fix the watcher lifecycle so notifications are reliable regardless of whether the caller is Claude in a tmux pane or Codex using exec_command. Prefer a supervised, singleton service with an explicit readiness signal over pidfile guessing.
close_reason: Launch agent-watch under launchd, add startup readiness checks, fix agent-state outside interactive tmux, and wire agent-watch binaries into install/update/doctor paths.

title: Make blocked agent-state transcript-only for Claude and Codex
description:
Follow-up from steez-a3e after fixing Codex working/idle detection.

Goal: eliminate pane-UI blocked detection in shared/steez/bin/agent-state and replace it with native artifact-backed detection from ~/.claude and ~/.codex.

What we already proved:
- Claude question blocks are explicit in transcript via AskUserQuestion and can be parsed directly.
- Codex question blocks are explicit when request_user_input is present and unresolved.
- Claude permission blocks are NOT explicit in the current live transcript shape: the blocked pane at session 6c921519-38a7-45af-8bc6-e0c33211eb22 only showed an unresolved Write tool_use with stop_reason=tool_use in /Users/stevedimakos/.claude/projects/-Users-stevedimakos-Projects-Personal-steez/6c921519-38a7-45af-8bc6-e0c33211eb22.jsonl, and the actual permission state only appeared in pane UI.
- For that live Claude permission block, the only other sidecar was /Users/stevedimakos/.claude/sessions/2841.json; there was no per-session folder or pending-tool sidecar yet.
- Codex unresolved function calls are ambiguous today because they can also mean interrupted/background work (for example unresolved wait_agent/spawn_agent/exec_command), so raw unresolved-call detection is not sufficient to label blocked:permission without a stronger native signal.
- Codex state_5.sqlite/logs_2.sqlite expose thread metadata and logs, but nothing we found yet cleanly said "waiting for approval".
- agent-history still has a stale binary path bug calling $HOME/.local/bin/agent-state instead of $HOME/.steez/bin/agent-state.

Scope:
- Audit ~/.claude and ~/.codex again for a real approval-wait marker that can drive blocked:permission without UI scraping.
- If the native artifacts do expose a durable signal, wire agent-state and agent-history to use it.
- If they do not, design the smallest durable runtime-written state source so blocked detection is still transcript/native-artifact only in practice, not screen-text heuristics.
- Fix the stale agent-history path bug as part of this work.

Acceptance:
- agent-state no longer depends on visible pane text for blocked:question.
- blocked:permission is sourced from a real native artifact or durable runtime state, not pane text heuristics.
- Evidence is documented with exact artifact paths and sample sessions for both Claude and Codex.
- agent-history --blocked works again for pane targets.
notes:
Implemented native blocked-state detection without pane-text scraping.

Evidence / verification:
- Claude permission: live pane %621, session 6c921519-38a7-45af-8bc6-e0c33211eb22, transcript /Users/stevedimakos/.claude/projects/-Users-stevedimakos-Projects-Personal-steez/6c921519-38a7-45af-8bc6-e0c33211eb22.jsonl. New durable runtime file is $HOME/.steez/agent-state/claude/<session>.json written by shared/steez/hooks/permission-state.sh on PermissionRequest and cleared on PostToolUse/PostToolUseFailure/UserPromptSubmit/Stop/SessionEnd plus SessionStart cleanup. Verified by feeding a real PermissionRequest-shaped payload into the hook: agent-state %621 --detail flipped to blocked:permission, then a Stop payload cleared it back to working.
- Claude question: pending AskUserQuestion transcript at /Users/stevedimakos/.claude/projects/-Users-stevedimakos-Projects-Personal-dotfiles-claude--claude-skills-steez/5d480841-d340-4274-a4e1-cf7508749c36.jsonl. Verified with agent-history <path> --blocked and with agent-state artifact_state helper returning blocked:question from transcript only.
- Codex permission: live pane %624, session 019d8701-fb20-7003-a5be-ec1042c650bb, transcript /Users/stevedimakos/.codex/sessions/2026/04/13/rollout-2026-04-13T09-22-27-019d8701-fb20-7003-a5be-ec1042c650bb.jsonl. Native signal is the matching thread tail in /Users/stevedimakos/.codex/log/codex-tui.log showing ToolCall: apply_patch followed by client: close with no later thread activity. Verified: agent-state mac:9.1 --detail => blocked:permission and agent-history %624 --blocked => apply_patch patch payload.
- Codex question: I could not find a preserved real transcript whose latest turn still ended on unresolved request_user_input; the historical real sessions on disk only proved the tool exists (for example /Users/stevedimakos/.codex/sessions/2026/03/05/rollout-2026-03-05T16-15-04-019cbfda-ab80-7403-9448-c8a5909f9eac.jsonl). I verified the parser path with a minimal Codex-format fixture shaped from that real schema under ~/.codex/sessions/2026/04/13/rollout-2026-04-13T23-59-59-019d-test-question.jsonl before deleting it: agent-history returned the question and agent-state artifact_state returned blocked:question.

Other fixes in scope:
- Restored agent-history to call $HOME/.steez/bin/agent-state instead of stale $HOME/.local/bin/agent-state.
- agent-history --blocked now handles current-turn Claude AskUserQuestion, Codex request_user_input, and Codex custom_tool_call pending patches.
- steez install / TUI install / doctor now include the new steez-permission-state.sh Claude hook symlink.
2026-04-13 administrative closeout:
- This bead is done, but the title/goal is now historically stale. The accepted design is not literally transcript-only for both runtimes.
- Final validated shape from steez-1ef and its follow-ups: Claude is native-artifact-first via the hook-backed sidecar at `~/.steez/agent-state/claude/<session>.json`, then transcript, with pane capture only as last fallback; Codex uses transcript/native artifacts for blocked question and blocked permission.
- The four-state matrix (`idle`, `working`, `blocked:question`, `blocked:permission`) was validated on main in steez-1ef with Claude evidence centered on `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/projects/-Users-stevedimakos-Projects-Personal-steez/6f0abc10-5c9b-4d36-b56e-b31a46801188.jsonl` and Codex evidence centered on `/Users/stevedimakos/.codex/sessions/2026/04/13/rollout-2026-04-13T12-59-02-019d87c8-4519-7ca1-bd2a-98180e48958b.jsonl` plus `/Users/stevedimakos/.codex/sessions/2026/04/13/rollout-2026-04-13T12-39-08-019d87b6-0d46-7cb2-9e40-42ca6b8d3751.jsonl`.
- Closing this bead as completed-with-refined-verdict rather than leaving it in progress.
close_reason: Completed. The original transcript-only framing was refined: Claude uses hook-backed sidecar artifacts, Codex uses transcript/native artifacts, and steez-1ef validated all four states on main.

title: Convert jira skill to subagent pattern
description:
Convert the jira skill (356 lines) from a skill that injects CLI reference into main context to a subagent that handles Jira operations in isolation. The main thread spawns the jira agent, the agent knows all acli syntax/gotchas/ADF templates internally, runs commands, and returns formatted results. The main thread never sees acli syntax.

Implementation:
1. Create agent definition at ~/.claude/agents/steez-jira.md with the jira domain knowledge (acli commands, ADF templates, gotchas, formatting)
2. Rewrite skills/jira/SKILL.md as a thin launcher that spawns the agent via the Agent tool
3. The agent needs: Bash (for acli/python3 commands), Read (for config discovery), Write (for temp JSON files), AskUserQuestion (for ambiguous requests)
4. Test by invoking /steez-jira and validating the agent handles operations and returns clean results to the main thread
5. Reload plugins after creating the agent so it gets picked up

Key files:
- Source: skills/jira/SKILL.md (current 356-line skill)
- Source: skills/jira/scripts/jira_worklog.py (worklog helper)
- Target: ~/.claude/agents/steez-jira.md (new agent definition)
- Target: skills/jira/SKILL.md (thin launcher replacement)

Verification: invoke /steez-jira, ask for open tickets, confirm the agent runs acli commands in its own context and returns formatted results to the main thread without leaking CLI reference material.
close_reason: Converted jira skill to subagent pattern. Agent definition at agents/steez-jira.md (acli commands, forms, worklog, auth diagnostics). Skill SKILL.md is now a thin launcher (356→73 lines) with trigger phrases and presentation rules. Tested end-to-end: acli, worklog, forms all working with API token auth. Agent spawns, runs queries in its own context, returns structured data. Skill tells main agent how to present results.

title: feat: /steez meta-skill — central router + default browse entry point
description:
Create a /steez meta-skill equivalent to /gstack. Central orchestrator that routes user intent to the correct steez-* skill, provides proactive mode control, voice/tone rules, completion protocol, and browse as default action.

## Open Questions (investigate first)
- How does gstack inject routing rules into CLAUDE.md? User-level (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) vs project-level (repo CLAUDE.md) vs configurable? Investigate gstack's implementation before deciding where steez routing lives.

## Section-by-section decisions (from /gstack SKILL.md review)
1. Frontmatter — KEEP (rewrite for steez, drop preamble-tier)
2. Preamble bash — KEEP (use existing steez preamble pattern, strip gstack onboarding flags)
3. Proactive mode behavior — KEEP (5 lines, swap skill name examples)
4. Skill prefix behavior — REMOVE (steez always uses steez-* prefix)
5. Upgrade check handler — REMOVE (steez deploys via stow)
6. Boil the Lake intro — REMOVE (gstack onboarding ceremony)
7. Telemetry opt-in flow — REMOVE (steez logs locally, no opt-in needed)
8. Proactive opt-in flow — REMOVE (config already exists)
9. Routing injection — KEEP routing rules, move to CLAUDE.md (see open question above)
10. Voice — TBD
11-onward — TBD (review paused to file bead and return to gstack removal)

## Depends on
- dot-0fw (tiered preamble system) informs which sections the meta-skill includes
close_reason: Claude Code's native skill matching against well-written frontmatter descriptions handles routing well enough for 21 skills. No daily pain point. The CLAUDE.md injection and routing table ideas were cargo-culted from gstack's architecture before steez's preamble system and skill descriptions were tuned. If disambiguation gaps emerge later, a CLAUDE.md routing section is simpler than a meta-skill.

title: permission-state.sh: call agent-eventsd evidence on hook events
description:
Extend `shared/steez/hooks/permission-state.sh` to call `agent-eventsd evidence` on `Stop`, `PermissionRequest`, and `PreToolUse(AskUserQuestion)` hook events, turning the 30s degraded-fallback wait into a sub-second notification.

Depends on steez-s4t (fake-harness must fire evidence for the primary-path test to cover hook semantics without real Claude).

## Why

Fix #2 of the hook-based fast-evidence plan. steez-2mn shipped the `evidence` CLI (the microphone). This bead wires the hook (the shouter). Together, Claude turn-end → agent-eventsd evidence → resolver → delivery in ~100ms instead of 30s.

## Scope

### `shared/steez/hooks/permission-state.sh`

After the sidecar write around line 62, add evidence dispatch:

- Map `hook_event_name` to canonical state:
  - `Stop` → `idle`
  - `PermissionRequest` → sidecar's `blocked_state` (already computed)
  - `PreToolUse` + `tool_name==AskUserQuestion` → `blocked:question`
- Skip evidence dispatch for clear events (`PostToolUse`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `SessionEnd`, `PostToolUseFailure`) — they already delete the sidecar; no evidence needed.
- Compute cursor: `cursor=$(wc -c < "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || echo 0)`
- Gate on `[ -n "$TMUX_PANE" ]` — non-tmux invocations skip silently.
- Fire-and-forget: `agent-eventsd evidence --pane "$TMUX_PANE" --state "$state" --transcript-cursor "$cursor" >/dev/null 2>&1 &` then disown.
- Do not block on the evidence call. Hook timeout is 5s in user's settings.json; evidence must stay well under that.

### `specs/agent-events.md`

Add the documentation drift fix alongside the behavior change (per CLAUDE.md: "update the matching spec in specs/ in the same commit").

1. Line 42 (Client mode): add `evidence` to the CLI list.
2. Under "Event surface" (line 66-73): add a bullet naming native-hook-driven CLI injection as a valid fast-evidence producer.

NOT in scope:
- Codex hook wiring (separate bead).
- Installer changes to settings.json (user already has hooks registered).
- Changes to agent-eventsd or the fake harness.

## TDD

Red → green → refactor.

### Red

Add runtime test in `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-agent-eventsd-runtime.sh`:

Scenario `claude_hook_fires_evidence_and_notification_arrives_within_2s`:
1. Spawn fake claude + spawner. Requires steez-s4t landed so fake-claude fires evidence.
2. `agent-send $target "hi"` (triggers full prearm→deliver→start).
3. Drive fake to idle via its fifo.
4. Assert: spawner pane receives exactly one delivery within 2s wall-clock (not 30s).
5. Assert: `agent-watch list` clears.

Prove it fails on HEAD before the hook wiring lands. Failure mode: test times out waiting for delivery (degraded fallback, 30s+). If steez-s4t is already in place, the test is gated entirely on the hook wiring — evidence won't fire from the hook because the hook doesn't call the CLI yet.

Also add a unit test for `permission-state.sh` itself: pipe canned hook payloads (Stop / PermissionRequest / PreToolUse+AskUserQuestion / PostToolUse), mock `agent-eventsd` on PATH with a recorder, assert the recorder saw the expected args (or no call) for each event.

### Green

Edit `permission-state.sh` minimally to make both tests pass. Update spec in the same commit.

### Refactor

Keep it simple. If the evidence dispatch is >10 lines, extract a helper inside the same file (no new files).

## Files

- `shared/steez/hooks/permission-state.sh`
- `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-agent-eventsd-runtime.sh`
- `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-permission-state-hook.sh` (new, if needed)
- `specs/agent-events.md`

## Acceptance

- New runtime test passes (<2s notification).
- Unit test for permission-state.sh passes.
- All existing tests stay green.
- Spec updated in the same commit.
- No regression in sidecar handling — `agent-state` still sees blocked states through the existing read path.
close_reason: permission-state.sh now shells out to 'agent-eventsd evidence' on Stop/PermissionRequest/PreToolUse(AskUserQuestion); runtime test asserts sub-2s spawner delivery under spec-default SILENCE_WINDOW_MS; unit test asserts event->state mapping and no-dispatch events; spec updated in the same commit (evidence in client-mode CLI list + native-hook CLI injection bullet under Event surface). All 12 agent test files green.

title: fake-harness: fire agent-eventsd evidence on state transitions
description:
Extend the fake claude/codex/ren/ren-codex agents so their state-transition commands (`state idle`, `state blocked:question`, `state blocked:permission`, `state blocked:unknown`) fire `agent-eventsd evidence` against their own pane, mimicking what real Claude/Codex hooks will do once `permission-state.sh` is wired.

## Why

This unblocks end-to-end tests for beads steez-<hook-claude> and steez-<hook-codex>. Real Claude hooks fire on turn-end; the fake has to simulate that so primary-path tests can prove sub-second notifications without real agents.

Today the fake only writes transcript entries. Tests that rely on hook-driven evidence cannot exist until the fake fires evidence. The evidence CLI from steez-2mn is already shipped — this bead just wires the fakes to call it.

## Scope

- `shared/steez/test/fakes/src/claude.impl.sh` (or equivalent) — after the transcript flush inside each state-transition handler, shell:
    `"$HOME/.steez/bin/agent-eventsd" evidence --pane "$TMUX_PANE" --state "$state" --transcript-cursor "$(wc -c < "$transcript" | tr -d ' ')" >/dev/null 2>&1 &`
- Same for codex.impl.sh / ren / ren-codex paths if they don't share the helper.
- `specs/fake-agent-harness.md` — add one sentence under 'Control surface' noting fakes fire evidence on state transitions, matching production hooks.

NOT in scope:
- Touching `shared/steez/hooks/` (those are beads A and B).
- Changing the fifo command surface — just extend existing handlers.
- agent-eventsd kernel changes.

## TDD

Red → green → refactor.

### Red

Add a test in `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-fake-harness.sh` (or new `test-fake-harness-evidence.sh`):
- Spawn fake claude.
- Create a watch manually (`agent-eventsd prearm` + `start`) with $TMUX_PANE=target.
- Drive the fake via its control fifo: `state idle ok`.
- Assert: spawner pane receives exactly one delivery within 2 seconds (the hook fast path).
- Assert: `agent-watch list` shows no live watch on target.
- Pre-fix, this must fail — fake flips transcript but never fires evidence, so the test times out on SILENCE_WINDOW_MS=30000 or succeeds only via degraded fallback. Prove it fails (or delays >2s) before writing the fix.

### Green

Add the evidence shell-out to the fake's state-idle path (and blocked:* paths). Fire-and-forget (background + discard output). No behavior change to transcript writes.

### Refactor

If claude.impl.sh and codex.impl.sh duplicate the call, hoist to a helper in `shared/steez/test/fakes/src/common.sh` or similar.

## Files

- `shared/steez/test/fakes/src/claude.impl.sh`
- `shared/steez/test/fakes/src/codex.impl.sh` (may or may not exist — check)
- `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-fake-harness.sh` OR new test file
- `specs/fake-agent-harness.md`

## Acceptance

- New test passes. Delivery lands in <2s on spawner pane.
- Existing fake-harness and agent-eventsd test suites still green (run both).
- Fake's existing auto-reply path still works (no regression).
- Spec updated.
close_reason: fake-harness fires agent-eventsd evidence on idle + blocked:question fifo transitions; red→green proven with new test-fake-harness-evidence.sh (pre-fix: 4068ms, post-fix: <2s via fast path); existing test-fake-harness.sh and test-agent-eventsd-runtime.sh stay green; full agent suite 11/11 green; spec updated. a3ebc3f.

title: agent-eventsd: add _cmd_evidence CLI for fast-path resolution
description:
Add `evidence` subcommand to `agent-eventsd` so external callers (hooks, fakes, manual debugging) can feed fast-path evidence into live watches. Today the canonical resolver (`watch_feed_evidence` at `shared/steez/bin/agent-eventsd:592`) is only reachable from inside the daemon (`watch_tick`, `watch_arm` buffer flush). No external entry point exists. Current CLI (`_eventsd_main:1207-1216`) exposes only `prearm|start|remove|list|status|serve|timeout-pending`.

## Why

This is fix #1 of the hook-based fast-evidence plan. Without a CLI entry point, hooks have nothing to call. Unblocks the Claude `Stop` / `PermissionRequest` hook integration that replaces the current 30s degraded-fallback delay with sub-second notifications.

## Scope (kernel only)

- Add `_cmd_evidence` handler in `shared/steez/bin/agent-eventsd`.
- Route it in `_eventsd_main` case statement.
- Update usage text.

NOT in scope for this bead:
- Wiring into `shared/steez/hooks/permission-state.sh` (separate bead).
- Codex hook wiring (separate bead).
- Spec update to `specs/agent-events.md` (separate bead after hook wiring lands).
- Fake-harness fifo command (separate bead).

## Contract

```
agent-eventsd evidence --pane P --state S [--transcript-cursor N] [--screen-hash H]
```

- **Flags:** `--pane` (required), `--state` (required, one of canonical states), `--transcript-cursor` (optional int), `--screen-hash` (optional string). At least one of cursor/hash must be passed — without either, the resolver's freshness gate (`agent-eventsd:646-654`) rejects all evidence and the command is a no-op by construction.
- **Behavior:**
  1. Call `_eventsd_auto_start_service` first (match `_cmd_prearm:944`). Hook callers may fire before any `prearm` has started the daemon.
  2. Look up pane's live watch via `watch_get_live "$pane"`. If none, exit 0 (fire-and-forget: stale hook, already resolved, or superseded is not an error).
  3. Extract `watch_id` from the live record.
  4. `seq=$(seq_next "$pane")`.
  5. Call `watch_feed_evidence --watch-id $wid --seq $seq --candidate-state $state --source fast` plus whichever of `--transcript-cursor` / `--screen-hash` was passed.
  6. Exit 0 regardless of resolver outcome.
- **Fire-and-forget semantics:** the caller (hook) has a 5s budget and doesn't care whether the watch existed or freshness passed. Non-zero exit only on bad flags (return 2, matching existing subcommand convention).

## TDD plan

Red → green → refactor.

### Red (new runtime test in `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-agent-eventsd-runtime.sh`)

Assertion target: end-to-end behavior, not structural. Must fail with the current CLI router (`unknown command: evidence`) AND pass only when the full chain works.

Test scenario `evidence_cli_resolves_armed_watch_and_fires_delivery`:
1. Set up fake spawner pane (existing harness pattern).
2. `agent-eventsd prearm --pane $P --spawner $S --label test --baseline-state working` → capture watch_id.
3. `agent-eventsd start --pane $P --watch-id $WID` → watch armed.
4. Capture `prearm_cursor` from the watch record via `agent-eventsd list` (primary surface, not state dir read).
5. Shell `agent-eventsd evidence --pane $P --state idle --transcript-cursor $((prearm_cursor + 100))`.
6. Poll for up to ~2s (service tick 0.5s): assert exactly one delivery landed on spawner pane via `tmux capture-pane` (spec rule 5: assert through public surface, not state files).
7. Assert `agent-watch list` shows no live watch on $P.

### Green

Add `_cmd_evidence` + route in `_eventsd_main`. Minimum code to pass.

### Refactor

Any dedup with `_cmd_prearm` around auto-start + flag parsing pattern. Keep behavior identical.

## Files

- `shared/steez/bin/agent-eventsd` (add handler, route, usage)
- `shared/steez/tests/agent/test-agent-eventsd-runtime.sh` (add test)

## Acceptance

- New runtime test passes.
- All existing `test-agent-eventsd.sh` and `test-agent-eventsd-runtime.sh` tests still pass.
- `bash shared/steez/bin/agent-eventsd --help` shows the new subcommand.
- No changes outside the two files listed.
close_reason: Added agent-eventsd evidence subcommand: _cmd_evidence handler + routing + usage. Red runtime test asserted unknown command then delivery on spawner + agent-watch list clear; passes green after handler lands. Kernel (36) and runtime (8) suites both clean. Kernel-only scope honored — no hooks, installer, or spec touched.

title: refactor: dist/ path drift in 3 runtime lookups (gstack-era math)
notes:
Three __dirname-based lookups compute paths that no longer exist in the steez layout but currently work via fallbacks. They're dead drift from the gstack restructure — they should be fixed or removed before a fallback silently masks a real bug.

1. src/core/server.ts:138 findBrowseBin: path.resolve(__dirname, '..', 'dist', 'browse') → src/dist/browse (dev) / $bunfs/.../dist/browse (compiled). Neither exists. Saved by ~/.steez/bin/browse + PATH fallbacks.

2. src/core/cli.ts:605 (sidebar-bin resolution): same pattern, same drift. Saved by process.execPath fallback in compiled mode.

3. src/core/browser-manager.ts:195 findExtensionPath: path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..', '..', 'extension') → steez/extension (doesn't exist in steez; was valid under gstack). Saved by ~/.claude/skills/gstack/extension fallback.

Fix: mirror the resolveServerScript pattern (check '$bunfs' marker, branch on dev vs compiled). Or remove the drifted candidates and rely explicitly on the fallback chain.

Tie-in: emitted by audit steez-ltc.
Mostly obsolete after steez-bql. 2/3 candidates are inside sidebar code (cli.ts:605 inside connect block, browser-manager.ts:195 findExtensionPath). Only server.ts:138 findBrowseBin may survive — and if it's only used by the sidebar-agent spawn, it becomes dead code and deletes entirely. Reassess after bql lands.

title: security: cookie-import + upload lack realpath check (symlink bypass)
notes:
Asymmetric path validation across file-reading commands:

- src/core/read-commands.ts:68-92 validateReadPath → uses realpathSync (correct)
- src/core/meta-commands.ts:18 SAFE_DIRECTORIES → duplicate copy WITHOUT realpathSync (different semantics)
- src/core/write-commands.ts:290-299 cookie-import path check → path.normalize + isPathWithin, NO realpath
- src/core/write-commands.ts:248-269 upload → trusts user-supplied path, NO safe-dir check at all

Attack: symlink inside /tmp/safe pointing to /etc/shadow passes cookie-import and meta-command screenshot checks but is blocked by validateReadPath. Upload has NO check and can send /etc/shadow to any form.

Fix: extract a single validateSafePath helper in src/core/platform.ts using realpathSync + isPathWithin; wire every file-reading command through it; decide explicit policy for upload (document 'can read anywhere' OR gate to safe dirs).

Tie-in: emitted by audit steez-ltc.
close_reason:
Unified path validation with realpath resolution.

COMMIT: 731b87b — security: unify path validation with realpath resolution (closes symlink bypass)

HELPER SIGNATURE:
  validateSafePath(userPath: string, allowedRoots?: string[], options?: { mustExist?: boolean }): string
  — returns the resolved real path on success
  — throws "Path must be within: ..." on symlink escape, traversal, or out-of-root
  — throws "File not found: <userPath>" only when mustExist=true AND safety passed
  — throws "Cannot resolve real path: ..." on non-ENOENT realpathSync failure (e.g. ENOTDIR)
  Safety check always runs before the existence check — a traversal to a nonexistent location is still a "Path must be within" rejection.

FILES CHANGED (all under shared/steez/browse/):
  - src/core/platform.ts: new validateSafePath + exported SAFE_DIRECTORIES; ancestor-walking resolver handles missing leaf and missing intermediate dirs
  - src/core/read-commands.ts: validateReadPath now delegates
  - src/core/meta-commands.ts: dropped duplicate SAFE_DIRECTORIES; validateOutputPath delegates
  - src/core/write-commands.ts: cookie-import (readFileSync uses resolved realFilePath) and upload (mustExist=true for every arg, setInputFiles uses resolved paths) both through validateSafePath
  - src/core/test/path-validation.test.ts: extended with validateSafePath suite — happy path, non-existent leaf, missing ancestor chain, mustExist throw, symlink leaf (mustExist and creation modes), symlinked parent, /tmp→/private/tmp on macOS, and the upload adversarial symlink-to-/etc/passwd test
  - src/core/test/commands.test.ts: updated 'cookie-import rejects path traversal' assertion to match unified error taxonomy (one line)

UPLOAD POLICY: Option A (gated to SAFE_DIRECTORIES). Upload is attacker-controllable input that gets sent to a remote form, so unrestricted upload is a realpath-bypass read primitive. Any legitimate upload can stage under /tmp or cwd. Documented in commit body.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Adversarial symlink-to-/etc/passwd test in path-validation.test.ts: 'blocks upload symlink bypass to /etc/passwd' — PASS (mirrors exact write-commands.ts upload validation call).
2. bun run test: 231/231 path-validation + commands tests PASS. 3 unrelated failures in server-auth.test.ts caused by sibling steez-bql worker's in-progress changes to server.ts (removing sidebar endpoints — outside my touch surface; brief flagged these files as off-limits).
3. All 10 pre-existing validateReadPath tests still PASS.
4. bun run build: success (bundle + node server).

No sidebar/cli/server/cookie-picker files touched. commands.test.ts staged/committed with only my 3-line assertion change; sibling's unrelated import-path fix left in working tree.

title: Create /tdd skill for slice-by-slice implementation loop
description: Design and validate a consolidated /tdd workflow skill in steez that takes an approved slice from a spec and runs an implementation loop with named failing tests, minimal green changes, refactor discipline, and verification evidence. The skill should optimize for small, independently verifiable slices and pair cleanly with the new /spec skill.
design:
Candidate workflow:
1. Read one approved slice from the design spec.
2. Validate the slice contract is complete: behavior, seam, fixture/harness, isolation, determinism, assertion contract, smoke budget, and verification command.
3. If the slice is underspecified, stop and hand it back to /spec.
4. Choose the smallest concrete first red test that fits the slice contract.
5. Add or run that one failing test and capture red evidence.
6. Implement the minimum code to pass.
7. Refactor while staying green.
8. Repeat red→green→refactor inside the same behavior and seam until the slice is done.
9. If execution reveals slice drift, a new seam, a new fixture model, or smoke-budget breach, stop and hand it back to /spec.
10. Run verification commands plus required verifier/browser QA.

Success criteria:
- The skill consumes an approved slice contract instead of a vague request.
- The skill rejects underspecified slices instead of inventing scope.
- /tdd owns the exact first red test, minimal green change, and refactor loop.
- /tdd does not choose test category or smoke allowance outside the slice contract.
- The output is evidence, not vibes.
notes:
Goal: one implementation-loop skill that consumes an approved slice contract from /spec.

Current ownership split:
- /spec owns what must be proved: behavior, seam, fixture/harness, isolation, determinism, assertion contract, smoke budget, and verification commands.
- /tdd owns how to prove it now: choose the smallest concrete first red test inside the slice, drive minimal green, refactor, and record evidence.

Rules to encode in the skill:
- Reject underspecified slices.
- Treat exact test naming and loop order as /tdd territory.
- Stay inside one approved slice at a time.
- Hand work back to /spec when the slice drifts during execution.

Open questions:
- How strict the test-first gate should be in repos with weak existing tests.
- When verifier and browser QA run inside versus after the loop.

title: Create unified /spec skill for software change planning
description: Design and validate a consolidated /spec workflow skill in steez. /spec is the front door for planned software changes. It should accept fuzzy or well-formed requests, challenge them enough to avoid bad plans, write a repo-local design-spec artifact, and stop when /tdd can implement approved slices without reconstructing the conversation. Keep /investigate separate for bug diagnosis and unclear root cause work.
design:
Current /spec workflow

1. Anchor to the current bead. Reuse the existing bead as the parent when one already matches the work. Create a parent planning bead only when no suitable bead exists.
2. Read bead context, repo context, and relevant code.
3. Run adaptive Phase 0 challenge:
   - default: XY check, carry cost
   - pull in as needed: pre-mortem, landscape check, smallest disprover
4. Decide one of three outcomes:
   - kill
   - answer directly
   - write a design spec
5. If writing a design spec, create a skeleton file at:
   - plans/<bead-id>-<topic-slug>-design-spec.md
6. Fill the sections the code and existing context already answer.
7. Ask the user only the smallest set of load-bearing questions that code cannot answer.
8. Update the design spec after each answer.
9. Repeat until /tdd could implement from the document without reconstructing the conversation.
10. Run a hard-failure lint gate before finishing.
11. Record the design-spec path on the parent bead and stop.

Success criteria
- A fuzzy or clear software-change request can enter /spec directly.
- /spec produces an execution-ready design spec for /tdd.
- /spec reduces front-door routing overhead by replacing the old planning stack.
notes:
CONSOLIDATED SOURCE OF TRUTH — supersedes prior notes.

Routing
- broken behavior or unclear root cause -> /investigate
- planned software change -> /spec
- implement an approved design-spec slice -> /tdd

Deprecated planning/front-door skills
- /workshop
- /office-hours
- /plan-ceo-review
- /plan-eng-review
- /plan-design-review
- /autoplan

Artifact contract
- /spec writes a flat repo-local planning artifact:
  plans/<bead-id>-<topic-slug>-design-spec.md
- example:
  plans/steez-wn6-spec-skill-design-spec.md
- keep specs/*.md reserved for shipped runtime specs only
- design specs are the execution contract for /tdd, not the canonical runtime spec

Design-spec section contract
Required sections
- Metadata
- Context
- Goals
- Non-goals
- Constraints & assumptions
- Requirements
- Proposed design
- Interface contracts
- Acceptance criteria
- Verification commands
- Implementation slices

Conditional sections
- Alternatives considered
- Cross-cutting concerns
- Rollout & rollback
- Open questions

Implementation-slice contract
Each slice must include:
- Slice ID
- Title
- Goal
- Boundary
- Files likely touched
- Red test name
- Green condition
- Refactor target
- Verification command

Bead ownership contract
- the markdown file is the source-of-truth planning artifact
- the bead is the control plane for lifecycle, ownership, and artifact pointer
- /spec defines slices in the design spec only
- /spec does not auto-create execution beads for every slice
- child execution beads later depend on the parent planning bead and cite spec_path plus slice_id

/tdd writeback contract
- /tdd does not edit the design spec
- /tdd executes one approved slice against that contract
- if execution reveals that a slice boundary, requirement, or acceptance condition should change, work hands back to /spec

Question rule
- never ask what code or repo context can answer
- ask only about requirements, preferences, tradeoffs, and unresolved decisions

Done gate
/spec is done when the design spec is an execution-ready contract for /tdd.
Hard-failure lint only:
- missing verification command
- missing failing test for any slice
- unowned open question
- missing boundary/interface contract
- over-length or bloated spec

title: agent-eventsd: pane close and daemon restart recovery
description: On pane close: pending closes without delivery; armed runs one final reconciliation over transcript bytes newer than the prearm cursor and falls back to blocked:unknown if non-terminal; draining delivery continues against spawner_pane. On daemon restart: pending closes as pending_timeout; armed gets one reconciliation pass before fast-path evidence is accepted; resolved re-delivers with the same watch_id; delivering demotes to delivery_failed and retries with the same watch_id; delivery_failed keeps its retry budget. Spec: specs/agent-events.md (Pane close and restart).
acceptance_criteria: First red test: restart_replays_resolved_with_same_watch_id_and_demotes_delivering_to_delivery_failed_preserving_retry_budget. Done when: acceptance #6 green; pane-close matrix and restart matrix each exercised from persisted fixtures; verifier PASS.

title: agent-eventsd: degraded fallback via agent-state
description: A watch is healthy while at least one fast observer produces fresh evidence. Silence past SILENCE_WINDOW_MS=30000 enters degraded. Degraded invokes agent-state <pane> every RECONCILE_INTERVAL_MS=5000; reconciliation feeds the same canonical resolver. INDETERMINATE_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 without a terminal state resolves blocked:unknown. Return to healthy clears the degraded timer; a later degraded episode starts a new window. Spec: specs/agent-events.md (Degraded fallback).
acceptance_criteria: First red test: silence_then_indeterminate_timeout_resolves_blocked_unknown_via_agent_state. Done when: acceptance #5 green; healthy-return-clears-timer and second-episode-resets-window both covered with fake clock; verifier PASS.
close_reason: Implemented degraded fallback via agent-state with passing focused tests; proves acceptance #5 from specs/agent-events.md.

title: agent-eventsd: delivery idempotency and bounded retries
description: watch_id is the idempotency key. Daemon persists resolved before invoking agent-deliver. Daemon is the sole notifier and never calls agent-send. delivery_failed retries with the same watch_id. MAX_DELIVERY_ATTEMPTS=5 caps retries; exhaustion closes watch as delivery_exhausted. Duplicate evidence after resolve produces no additional notification. Spec: specs/agent-events.md (delivering; Delivery contract; Default timers).
acceptance_criteria: First red test: one_watch_id_produces_one_logical_notification_across_duplicate_evidence_and_bounded_retries. Done when: acceptance #4 green; persist-before-deliver gate asserted; retry budget exhaustion closes with delivery_exhausted; verifier PASS.

title: agent-eventsd: live-watch supersession
description: A new turn.prearm on a pane with an existing live (pending or armed) watch creates the new watch immediately and marks the prior watch superseded without waiting for any draining delivery to finish. title: agent-eventsd: canonical resolver with freshness and baseline rules
description: Pure resolver over (watch, evidence) -> {resolve(state), keep_open, ignore}. Fresh iff seq > prearm_seq AND (transcript bytes after prearm cursor OR screen capture differs from prearm hash). working keeps open, never resolves. title: Make Codex blocked:question reproducible from steez agent-spawn
description:
Why this exists
Live validation in steez-cnq could not produce a real Codex blocked:question state from spawned sessions. A forced `request_user_input` prompt in pane %646 returned '`request_user_input` is unavailable in Default mode, so I can’t use it here.' That means current spawned Codex sessions do not expose the tool path that agent-state expects for blocked:question.

Scope
- Determine the smallest supported way to launch a Codex session that can emit `request_user_input` (or explicitly document that current spawned Codex sessions cannot).
- If supported, wire that path into spawn/validation so blocked:question can be live-tested.
- If not supported, codify the limitation so Codex question-state claims do not overpromise.

Acceptance criteria
- We have a durable verdict on whether spawned Codex sessions can ever produce blocked:question in this setup.
- If yes, a live reproduction path exists and is documented.
- If no, the limitation is documented in the relevant spawn/state guidance.
notes:
2026-04-13 request_user_input reproducibility

Verdict
- Smallest durable fix is to launch spawned Codex sessions with `--enable default_mode_request_user_input`.
- That is now wired into `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/skills/spawn-agent/scripts/spawn.sh`.
- No follow-up bead needed for blocked:question reproducibility in the current setup.

Local capability check
- `codex --version` => `codex-cli 0.120.0`
- `codex features list` included:
  - `default_mode_request_user_input  under development  false`
  - `collaboration_modes              removed            true`
- Conclusion: current spawns were landing in Default mode with the tool disabled unless the feature flag was turned on at launch.

Exact baseline failure command
- `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/skills/spawn-agent/scripts/spawn.sh new-session --session steez-0sd-default-125604 --model codex --prompt "Use the request_user_input tool now. Ask exactly this question and nothing else: 'Which color should I report back: red or blue?' Do not ask in plain text."`

Baseline failure evidence
- Spawn output: `SELF=%650 TARGET=%651`, `MODEL=codex`, `PROMPT_SENT`, `WORKING`, `WATCHED=%651 SPAWNER=%650 BASELINE=working`
- `~/.steez/bin/agent-state %651 --detail` => state=`idle`, session_id=`019d87c5-96b9-7862-b6b3-153b33ec9fd6`

title: Make agent-history --blocked fall back to visible Claude pane when transcript lags
notes:
2026-04-13 cold-start context:
- This bead is the Claude follow-up from steez-tjd. steez-tjd validated vanilla Claude state detection live and is now closed. Fresh evidence from that bead must be treated as the starting point here.
- Problem: vanilla Claude can show real blocked UI before the transcript records it. During live validation, pane %629 (session_id `01a47140-b38e-490e-a146-8bb1f1ef24d0`, transcript `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/projects/-Users-stevedimakos-Projects-Personal-steez/01a47140-b38e-490e-a146-8bb1f1ef24d0.jsonl`) displayed a real AskUserQuestion chooser while the transcript still ended at ToolSearch + tool_result and `agent-history %629 --blocked` returned `no pending tool use found`. Pane %628 (session_id `6f0abc10-5c9b-4d36-b56e-b31a46801188`, transcript `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/projects/-Users-stevedimakos-Projects-Personal-steez/6f0abc10-5c9b-4d36-b56e-b31a46801188.jsonl`) displayed real permission UI while the transcript still lagged the live block.
- Current code state: `agent-state` now reports Claude `blocked:question` / `blocked:permission` by matching visible pane UI first at `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/shared/steez/bin/agent-state:339-352`. That was a fast live fix, but it is not the desired final design. The user explicitly wants tmux pane capture to be a last resort, not the primary method.
- Desired end state for Claude: artifact-first blocked-state detection. Pane-visible capture only after stronger artifacts fail.
- Verified direction: use a Claude hook-backed sidecar state file as the primary real-time signal for blocked states. The existing permission sidecar pattern is already in `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/shared/steez/hooks/permission-state.sh`. Claude debug logs show `PreToolUse` exists (example: `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/debug/dde481f7-fb9f-4f84-a8c3-73aeadda7db4.txt:70` shows `Getting matching hook commands for PreToolUse with query: Read`). Verifier verdict was PASS: extending the sidecar pattern is the cleanest durable fix.
- Planned implementation shape: (1) extend the Claude hook path so vanilla Claude sessions write a sidecar file under `~/.steez/agent-state/claude/<session_id>.json` for both `blocked:question` and `blocked:permission`; (2) have `agent-state` consult that sidecar before transcript parsing and before any pane-visible fallback; (3) have `agent-history --blocked` consult the same sidecar before transcript parsing; (4) keep pane capture only as the final fallback.
- Current machine hook wiring note: `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/settings.json` already has SessionStart via `steez-session-start.sh`, but does not yet wire `steez-permission-state.sh` / blocked-state hooks. This bead should make the Claude hook path and installer guidance coherent for vanilla Claude sessions.
- Adjacent completed fix from steez-tjd: `agent-send` interoperability bug was already fixed by restoring `/Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/shared/steez/bin/agent-deliver` to use `$HOME/.steez/bin/agent-state`. Do not spend time rediscovering that.
- Completion target for this bead: a fresh session should be able to read this bead, understand why transcript-only fails for Claude blocked states, know that pane capture must become last resort, and implement the hook-backed sidecar path without referring back to steez-tjd except for extra evidence.
Implemented hook-backed Claude blocked-state sidecars for both PermissionRequest and PreToolUse AskUserQuestion, then switched agent-state and agent-history to prefer that sidecar before falling back to transcript or pane capture. Installer hook registration now validates the full hook set and prints coherent snippets, with coverage in internal/installer/hooks_test.go. Verification: bash -n on the touched scripts, go test ./internal/installer/..., temp-home hook writes/clears, agent-history --blocked returning the sidecar question, and a fake tmux Claude pane returning blocked:permission from agent-state.
Live Claude verification on 2026-04-13 found two more real bugs and both are now fixed locally. (1) agent-state rejected panes where tmux pane_pid was claude directly instead of shell->claude; fixed by recognizing direct claude/codex/node pane processes before falling back to child scan. (2) permission sidecars could go stale after cancel because the transcript advanced but the state file remained; fixed by ignoring sidecars once transcript mtime is newer than requested_at + 0.5s. Also corrected the hook so AskUserQuestion PermissionRequest events are tagged blocked:question. Fresh vanilla Claude pane %643 now verified live: idle, working->idle, working->blocked:question with agent-history --blocked returning the live question, question cleared back to idle, working->blocked:permission with agent-history --blocked returning the pending Read request, permission canceled back to idle.
close_reason: Hook-backed Claude blocked-state detection is fixed locally and verified live in a fresh vanilla Claude pane for idle, working, blocked:question, blocked:permission, and return-to-idle after question answer or permission cancel. Follow-up smoke coverage is split to a dedicated bead.

title: Validate agent-spawn and agent-state for Codex sessions
description:
Why this exists
Codex agent-state/agent-spawn validation uses a different runtime and different artifacts than Claude. We need a clean, isolated validation for Codex sessions so we know which states are reliable from transcript + codex-tui log alone, whether SessionStart hook wiring is enough, and whether agent-spawn/agent-send behave correctly for real Codex panes.

Scope
- Reproduce fresh live Codex sessions launched through steez agent-spawn.
- Validate pane recognition, transcript resolution, and agent-send interoperability for Codex panes.
- Validate idle, working, blocked:question, and blocked:permission detection against real UI + Codex artifacts.
- Confirm exactly how much blocked:permission depends on codex-tui log tailing and whether any stronger runtime state source is needed.
- Record exact pane ids, session ids, transcript paths, thread/log evidence, and any mismatch cases.
- If behavior is wrong, identify the smallest next change, but do not fix Claude in this bead.

Acceptance criteria
- Live validation exists for Codex idle, working, blocked:question, and blocked:permission.
- We have a clear verdict on whether current Codex detection is durable enough as-is.
- We have a clear verdict on whether agent-spawn and agent-send correctly support Codex panes.
- Evidence is recorded with exact artifact paths and observed pass/fail results.
notes:
2026-04-13 Codex live validation in /Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez

Verdict
- Pane recognition works.
- agent-send works against real Codex panes.
- idle + working detection works after the first real turn.
- blocked:question is not live-reproducible in the current spawned Codex environment.
- blocked:permission is currently misdetected as working.

Fresh no-turn spawn: idle only via screen heuristics
- Spawn command: /Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/skills/spawn-agent/scripts/spawn.sh new-session --session steez-cnq-fresh-124124 --model codex

- Spawned session: steez-cnq-idle-123618
- Pane: %645
- Before first turn: ~/.steez/bin/agent-state %645 --detail returned session_id=null and transcript_path=null.
- Sent via ~/.steez/bin/agent-send %645:
  - "Use the shell to run 'sleep 8' and then reply with exactly READY."
- After send:

  - tmux show-options -p -v -t %645 @transcript_path now matched that transcript path
- ~/.steez/bin/agent-history %645 --last => prompt was the exact sent string, response was READY.
- Transcript tail showed unresolved tool execution followed by function_call_output and task_complete.
- Conclusion: agent-send interoperates correctly with real Codex panes, and once the pane has taken one real turn, transcript/session resolution is durable.

blocked:question: NOT live-reproducible in current spawned Codex sessions
- Spawned session: steez-cnq-question-123739
- Pane: %646
- Sent via ~/.steez/bin/agent-send %646:
  - "Use the request_user_input tool now. Ask exactly this question and nothing else: 'Which color should I report back: red or blue?' Do not ask in plain text."
- Observed result:

That means current Codex blocked:question detection is not validated live here. The smallest next change is a spawn path that can launch a Codex session with request_user_input enabled (likely a Plan-mode/alternate collaboration-mode path).

blocked:permission: LIVE UI + transcript say blocked, agent-state says working
- Spawned session: steez-cnq-perm-123852
- Pane: %647
- Because /Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/skills/spawn-agent/scripts/spawn.sh:218 hardcodes `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`, I had to relaunch plain Codex in the spawned pane to make approval prompts possible:
  - sent C-c in %647
  - relaunched: codex -a on-request -s workspace-write
- Sent via ~/.steez/bin/agent-send %647:
  - "Use the shell to run exactly: touch /Users/stevedimakos/steez-cnq-permission-check.txt . Do not ask me for any extra clarification."
- Visible UI tail in %647 showed a real approval prompt:
  - "Would you like to run the following command?"
  - reason text about creating the file outside the workspace sandbox
  - numbered options 1/2/3
- File check:
  - /Users/stevedimakos/steez-cnq-permission-check.txt => MISSING
- Transcript/session:
  - session_id=019d87b6-0d46-7cb2-9e40-42ca6b8d3751
  - transcript_path=/Users/stevedimakos/.codex/sessions/2026/04/13/rollout-2026-04-13T12-39-08-019d87b6-0d46-7cb2-9e40-42ca6b8d3751.jsonl
- ~/.steez/bin/agent-history %647 --blocked =>
  - tool=exec_command
  - cmd=touch /Users/stevedimakos/steez-cnq-permission-check.txt
  - justification asked for permission outside the workspace sandbox
  - sandbox_permissions=require_escalated
- But ~/.steez/bin/agent-state %647 --detail returned state=working for 20 straight polls while the approval menu was on screen.
- Codex log evidence for thread_id=019d87b6-0d46-7cb2-9e40-42ca6b8d3751:
  - `ToolCall: exec_command {"cmd":"touch /Users/stevedimakos/steez-cnq-permission-check.txt", ... "sandbox_permissions":"require_escalated"}` is present in ~/.codex/log/codex-tui.log
  - there is NO later `client: close` line for that thread after the ToolCall line
- Root cause:
  - /Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/shared/steez/bin/agent-state:257-314 only returns true when the newest matched thread log line contains `client: close` and an earlier matched line contains `ToolCall:`.
  - /Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/shared/steez/bin/agent-state:347-348 then maps Codex custom_tool_call to blocked:permission only if that log helper returns true.
  - In the live permission prompt above, transcript + UI clearly show a blocked permission state, but the log pattern the helper expects never materializes, so the pane is misclassified as working.
- Conclusion: current Codex blocked:permission detection is not durable enough as-is. It fails on a real approval prompt.

Overall verdict
- agent-spawn + agent-state are good enough for Codex pane recognition and for working/idle after the first user turn.
- SessionStart hook wiring is not sufficient to populate pane metadata for a brand-new no-turn Codex pane; state falls back to screen heuristics until the first real turn creates transcript/session data.
- agent-send works correctly with Codex panes.
- blocked:question is currently untestable live from spawned Codex sessions in this environment because request_user_input is unavailable in Default mode.
- blocked:permission is currently wrong and depends on an overly specific codex-tui log-tail heuristic that missed a real approval prompt.

Smallest next changes
- Add a way to spawn a Codex session that can actually call request_user_input, or accept that blocked:question cannot be validated in current Default-mode Codex panes.
- Fix Codex blocked:permission detection to key off the actual pending transcript/tool state (or a stronger runtime signal) instead of requiring the current `ToolCall` + latest `client: close` log pattern.
Follow-up beads filed from this validation: steez-mqe (Codex blocked:permission fix), steez-0sd (Codex blocked:question reproducibility).
close_reason: Live Codex validation completed. Evidence recorded in notes with exact pane/session/transcript/log paths; follow-up beads filed as steez-mqe and steez-0sd for the gaps uncovered.

title: Validate agent-spawn and agent-state for Claude sessions
description:
Why this exists
Claude agent-state/agent-spawn validation is currently muddied by two separate realities: transcript-derived state and optional Claude hook wiring. We need a clean, end-to-end validation for vanilla Claude sessions so we know exactly which states are reliable without extra runtime help, which states require the permission-state hook, and whether agent-spawn/agent-send are correctly interoperating with agent-state for real Claude panes.

Scope
- Reproduce fresh live Claude sessions launched through steez agent-spawn.
- Validate pane recognition, transcript resolution, and agent-send interoperability for vanilla Claude panes.
- Validate idle, working, blocked:question, and blocked:permission detection against real UI + transcript artifacts.
- Explicitly separate what works transcript-only from what only works with the new permission-state hook.
- Record exact pane ids, session ids, transcript paths, and any mismatch cases.
- If behavior is wrong, identify the smallest next change, but do not fix Codex in this bead.

Acceptance criteria
- Live validation exists for Claude idle, working, blocked:question, and blocked:permission.
- We have a clear verdict on whether Claude needs the new permission-state hook for any state.
- We have a clear verdict on whether agent-spawn and agent-send correctly support vanilla Claude panes.
- Evidence is recorded with exact artifact paths and observed pass/fail results.
notes:
2026-04-13 follow-up findings:
- Validation target should be plain vanilla `claude`, but current `skills/spawn-agent/scripts/spawn.sh` does not launch vanilla Claude. It hardcodes `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` at /Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/skills/spawn-agent/scripts/spawn.sh:214-218, which suppresses the very permission prompt path this bead needs to validate.

- `agent-send` interoperability has an adjacent bug unrelated to hooks: /Users/stevedimakos/Projects/Personal/steez/shared/steez/bin/agent-deliver:37 points AGENT_STATE at `$HOME/.local/bin/agent-state`, but this machine only has /Users/stevedimakos/.steez/bin/agent-state. That explains why prior live Claude send attempts failed even when `agent-state` itself recognized the pane.
2026-04-13 live validation after vanilla-Claude retest:
- Vanilla Claude pane %628 (tmux mac:8.1) launched with raw `claude`, not spawn.sh. `agent-state %628 --detail` resolved session_id `6f0abc10-5c9b-4d36-b56e-b31a46801188` and transcript `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/projects/-Users-stevedimakos-Projects-Personal-steez/6f0abc10-5c9b-4d36-b56e-b31a46801188.jsonl`. - Vanilla Claude question block: pane %629 (tmux mac:9.1), session_id `01a47140-b38e-490e-a146-8bb1f1ef24d0`, transcript `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/projects/-Users-stevedimakos-Projects-Personal-steez/01a47140-b38e-490e-a146-8bb1f1ef24d0.jsonl`. Sent via fixed `~/.steez/bin/agent-send %629 ... AskUserQuestion ...`. Polls: `working` through sample 10, then `blocked:question` on samples 11-12. Visible UI showed the chooser (`Which option would you like for this test?`, options A/B, `Enter to select`). Transcript tail still stopped at ToolSearch/tool_result lines 8-11 and had not yet written the AskUserQuestion tool_use, so transcript-only remained insufficient in the blocked moment. `agent-history %629 --blocked` still returned `no pending tool use found` during the visible block.
- Vanilla Claude permission block: pane %628 stayed in `blocked:permission` when the screen showed a real approval UI (`Read file ... Do you want to proceed?` and later `Overwrite file ... Do you want to overwrite ...?`). Transcript tail showed unresolved Write + tool_use_error at lines 30-32 for the failed write, but the live permission prompt itself was not yet represented when the UI blocked. There is still no Claude permission sidecar file for this session under `/Users/stevedimakos/.steez/agent-state/claude`; current machine wiring still lacks `steez-permission-state.sh` in `/Users/stevedimakos/.claude/settings.json`.
- Verdict after code change: vanilla Claude no longer needs the permission hook for current-state detection in tmux panes because `agent-state` now falls back to visible pane UI for blocked question/permission when transcript artifacts lag.
